name: United Kingdom
overview: The UK retained the GDPR post-Brexit as the 'UK GDPR', supplemented by the Data Protection Act 2018 and PECR for e-privacy. The Data (Use and Access) Act 2025 (DUAA) made targeted reforms — recognised legitimate interests, relaxed automated decision-making rules, smart data schemes and ICO restructuring into an Information Commission — while preserving EU adequacy, which the Commission renewed in 2025.

legal_instruments.title: Data Protection Act 2018
legal_instruments.summary: Supplements and tailors the UK GDPR: exemptions, special-category conditions, law-enforcement processing (Part 3), intelligence services (Part 4), and ICO enforcement powers.

legal_instruments.title: UK GDPR (Retained Regulation (EU) 2016/679)
legal_instruments.issuing_authority: UK Parliament (retained EU law, as amended)
legal_instruments.summary: The GDPR as retained in UK law post-Brexit, amended by the DPPEC Regulations 2019 and the Data (Use and Access) Act 2025.

legal_instruments.title: Data (Use and Access) Act 2025
legal_instruments.summary: Reform act: recognised legitimate interests list, purpose-compatibility clarifications, relaxed Art. 22 automated-decision rules (except special-category data), subject-access 'stop the clock', smart data schemes, digital verification services, and conversion of the ICO into the Information Commission. Commencing in phases via regulations.

legal_instruments.title: Privacy and Electronic Communications (EC Directive) Regulations 2003 (PECR)
legal_instruments.summary: E-privacy rules on marketing, cookies and communications security; DUAA raised PECR fines to UK GDPR levels and added limited cookie-consent exceptions.

developments.title: European Commission renews UK adequacy decisions
developments.summary: The Commission adopted renewed UK adequacy decisions (to December 2031) after assessing the Data (Use and Access) Act, preserving free EU-UK data flows.

developments.title: Data (Use and Access) Act 2025 receives Royal Assent
developments.summary: DUAA became law after extended parliamentary ping-pong over AI/copyright amendments, with staged commencement through 2025-26.

penalties: Up to GBP 17.5 million or 4% of worldwide annual turnover (whichever higher); PECR penalties aligned to the same maxima by the DUAA.
cross_border: UK adequacy regulations (including a US 'data bridge' extension to the DPF), Art. 46 safeguards (IDTA / UK Addendum to EU SCCs) with transfer risk assessments, or derogations. DUAA introduced a 'data protection test' for future adequacy findings.
